name: Centraliza Ayuda
db_description:
##about
web para enlazar y centralizar iniciativas en pro a momentos post terremoto

Se aceptan pull request

analysis.summary: A pure static-site directory aggregating 130+ Venezuelan earthquake relief resources. Hosted on GitHub Pages with no backend. All data is embedded as a JS const array (data_source_json.js) maintained via a Python merge/dedup script. The frontend uses clean vanilla JS with a class-based MVC-like structure, debounced text search, and tag filtering. Community submissions go through a Google Form then a PR-based git pipeline. The project is genuinely live and active, with real curated content across 13 categories.

architecture.pattern: Static site with vanilla JS class-based MVC pattern
architecture.orm_or_db_layer: None — data is a hardcoded JS const SOURCES array in data_source_json.js, built by merge_sources.py from a JSON flat file
architecture.api_design: No live API; ApiManager class has the actual fetch calls commented out behind a 'Future backend integration' comment; baseUrl is hardcoded to http://localhost:3000 but never called in production
architecture.separation_of_concerns: Good — Manager orchestrates event wiring, ApiManager handles data access, View renders results, SideBar/SearchText/TagSelectedZone are separate files each with a single class
architecture.modularity: 8 JS files each containing one class, 5 separate CSS files per component; Python tooling for content management is separate in data_source_helper/
notable_patterns: Private class fields (#) throughout all JS classes
URL normalization and dedup logic in Python merge script (strips utm_, fbclid, trailing slashes, www)
Keyboard accessibility with Enter/Space key handlers on tag buttons
ARIA labels and aria-live on results list

code_organization.directory_structure: Flat but logical: css/ per-component stylesheets, js/ per-class files, data_source_helper/ for content tooling. No build step needed.
code_organization.naming_quality: Clear and consistent — files named after their class (side_bar.js, tags_selected_zone.js, result_item_view_builder.js). Spanish naming in data fields matches the domain.
code_organization.documentation_quality: README is minimal but accurate — only covers how to add new sources via merge_sources.py. No JSDoc, no architecture overview.
code_organization.reasoning: The codebase is clean and well-structured for its scope. The class-based vanilla JS with private fields shows deliberate design. The main gap is zero inline or API documentation.

production_readiness.secrets_handling: N/A — no secrets; static site with no server-side code
production_readiness.security_notes: Static site has minimal attack surface. No XSS risk from current data. The commented-out backend integration has no auth or CSRF planning visible.
production_readiness.scalability_notes: Trivially scalable as a static CDN-served site. Adding the planned backend (localhost:3000) will require a full new architecture decision.
production_readiness.reasoning: The site functions and is live, but it is entirely static — content updates require a developer git commit cycle. The hardcoded localhost:3000 API base URL and fully commented-out fetch code confirm the backend is unbuilt. No tests, no CI, no deploy config.

maturity.is_real_or_boilerplate: Real — 130 curated entries with descriptions, 54 commits, 3 contributors, actively adding new sources as of June 29 2026
maturity.reasoning: This is genuine, actively maintained content — not scaffolding. The Python tooling for content management is thoughtful. The absence of tests and CI is the main maturity gap.

viability.reasoning: Already live on GitHub Pages and serving real users as a go-to directory. The 130+ real resource links provide immediate value to earthquake victims. However, the fully static architecture means rapid content updates require developer involvement, and the planned backend integration is entirely absent. Community contribution is gated through a Google Form plus a PR review cycle.

key_features: Curated directory of 130+ Venezuelan earthquake relief resources across 13 tag categories
Debounced text search (300ms) matching name, description, and tags
Tag-based sidebar filtering with multi-select and active tag display zone
Python merge_sources.py script with URL normalization and deduplication for content management
Google Form link for community resource submissions
Keyboard-accessible tag interactions with ARIA attributes for accessibility
analysis.merge_potential: High — this is already a meta-aggregator that lists other hackathon projects (id 100, 103, 115). It could naturally absorb links to winning Build4Venezuela projects and serve as a persistent discovery layer. Its PR-based data pipeline is low-friction for integrators.
red_flags: API baseUrl hardcoded to http://localhost:3000 — the future backend is unbuilt
All fetch/API calls in api_manager.js are commented out behind a TODO comment
Content updates require a full git commit cycle — no live CMS or admin panel
No tests of any kind (unit or integration)
Adding new resources requires developer intervention despite Google Form submission pathway

evaluation.real_problem_reasoning: After an earthquake, fragmented information is one of the biggest killers of effective response. Victims and families searching for missing persons, shelter, or donation channels have to hunt across dozens of uncoordinated sources. This project aggregates 130 real, curated resources across 7 critical categories — missing persons portals, shelters, health services, donation hubs, maps, and volunteer coordination — into one searchable directory. That is exactly the right tool for the right moment. The need is real and the content is real.
evaluation.live_demo_notes: The site is live on GitHub Pages and the data file (data_source_json.js) is publicly accessible and confirmed to contain 130 real resource entries. However, the entire directory is rendered client-side via vanilla JS — WebFetch sees only the static HTML shell (header, form links, contact email) with no visible resources. A real browser renders the full searchable directory with tag filters and debounced search. The demo is working for real users but cannot be verified by headless/non-JS fetching tools. The main page gives no fallback non-JS content, which is an accessibility gap.

diffusion.recommended_angle: Position it as the central nervous system of Venezuela earthquake aid: '130+ recursos de ayuda al terremoto en Venezuela, en un solo lugar — búscalos por categoría o texto libre.' Emphasize the breadth (missing persons, refugios, salud, donaciones, mapas) and that it is open to community contributions. Call for organizations to submit their resource via the Google Form. Works best as a retweet/share anchor — give the URL and one screenshot of the directory in action.
gaps: no demo video showing the search and tag filtering in action
no screenshots committed to the repo or linked from README
no branding — site has no logo, icon, or distinctive visual identity
no confirmed real-user testimonials or usage metrics
README is minimal — no elevator pitch or impact statement that can be copy-pasted for promotion

evaluation.one_line_pitch: 130+ recursos de ayuda al terremoto Venezuela — personas desaparecidas, refugios, salud y donaciones, todo en un directorio con búsqueda y filtros, abierto a la comunidad.
